agent_notes: Books & Coffee is the tightest Camden category: many excellent independent bookshops lack on-site or literally next-door independent coffee with seating, so only London Review Bookshop / St. JOHN cleared the PAINTS rule.

Creative Walks are limited to Regent's Park, Primrose Hill, and permitted Regent's Canal stretches from the PAINTS v6 safety list; Hampstead Heath was deliberately excluded because it is not on the approved list.
Several venues have official hours and booking pages but do not explicitly confirm group walk-ins for 8-12; these are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: DesignMyNight lists Tue 18:00-21:00, Wed/Thu 18:00-22:00, Fri 12:00-22:00, Sat 12:00-21:00, Sun 18:00-21:00; Tock showed live availability on 2026-07-07. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm July event date and no private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations available via Tock; walk-in policy for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Demotion avoided because live booking inventory suggests operational flexibility, but host should call.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable only with early slot or heads-up; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Small neighbourhood wine bar/restaurant; group should use an early Friday/Saturday or Sunday slot.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 12:30-15:00 or Sunday 18:00 before peak dinner.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is a small, specific north-London wine room rather than a generic bar: seasonal plates, natural-wine energy, and a founder story rooted in proper hospitality. It rewards the slight effort of York Way. It gives Show & Tell a table with texture, bottles, and conversation hooks.
venues.anecdote_story: Kentishtowner reports it opened in 2021 as a wine bar, shop and deli founded by four north London friends with backgrounds including Noble Rot, The Marksman and 40FT Brewery.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Small plates, wine bar/shop, natural/low-intervention leaning wine list.
venues.safety_notes: York Way is busy and well lit toward King's Cross/Caledonian Road; recommend arrivals before late evening and direct routing to Caledonian Road or King's Cross.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min by Google Maps walking search; verify exact route before event.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small room and strong press mean peak dinner can be tight; group walk-in tolerance needs a call.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists pub table bookings Mon-Sat 12:00-00:00 and Sun 12:00-22:30; dining room Mon-Thu 18:00-21:30, Fri 17:30-21:30, Sat 12:00-14:30 and 18:00-21:30, Sun 12:00-19:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official event page says walk in to grab a spot at the bar; dining bookings are up to 8 and larger parties use private dining, so 8-12 walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Tier B because the table model is not fully PAINTS-walk-in robust.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Bar walk-ins possible; seated group 8-12 likely needs booking/private room.
venues.capacity_notes: Public house plus dining room and private room; host should avoid dinner peak.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 14:30-17:00 pub slot or Sunday early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: A polished Kentish Town pub with a serious kitchen and grown-up wine list, but still public-house enough for informal conversation. It is visually strong without feeling staged. The operational caveat is group seating rather than taste.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself as a new public house with private dining on Kentish Town Road; the fire-led dining menu supplies a strong food-and-place story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: British seasonal food cooked over flame, wine, pub drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Kentish Town Road is a main route with good transport; late-night departures should be directed to Kentish Town station.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Too popular for spontaneous 8-12 seating at dinner; cap as Tier B unless PAINTS books.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-midnight, Sun 12:00-22:30; kitchen Mon-Fri 12:00-15:00 and 18:00-21:45, Sat-Sun 13:00-21:45.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says reservations only for groups of 10+; casual walk-ins implied but group 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 with host call; upstairs function room exists but may trigger booking.
venues.capacity_notes: Official site mentions 60-person upstairs function room, conservatory and garden.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Grade II listed backstreet free-house with genuine local affection and a proper pub texture. It is less fashion-forward than the wine bars, but its story and room character are excellent for Show & Tell. The Thai kitchen and Victorian fabric give the group more to talk about than a standard pint stop.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says the pub has been part of Kentish Town since 1868; press has also highlighted its local rescue/heritage reputation.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft ales, pub drinks, Thai kitchen, conservatory and garden.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet residential backstreets north of Kentish Town; arrivals should be routed from Kentish Town station in pairs after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a natural-wine specialist; group policy must be confirmed for non-booked PAINTS format.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon closed except bank holidays; Tue 15:00-23:00; Wed/Thu 12:00-00:00; Fri-Sat 12:00-01:00; Sun 12:00-22:00, with food windows.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings page exists; walk-in acceptance for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Tier B because group walk-in confidence is not explicit.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable outside peak if host calls; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent free house with food, gin/craft beer positioning and private-event capacity signals.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Camden backstreet independent pub with enough food and drink identity to support a hosted table. It has character without the full Camden Market tourist crush. It is better as an operational backup than a flagship.
venues.anecdote_story: Venue brands itself as Camden's independent free house; Kentishtowner has covered its Pass Time kitchen and cosy-corner appeal.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Gin, craft beer, pub food, late-night pub atmosphere.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Camden Road Overground and Camden Town; recommend main-road routing after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 14 min; no qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min that clearly beats PAINTS floor, so primary remains Tier B.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup outside strict 10-min radius; do not run as Tier A without a nearer confirmed backup.

venues.opening_hours: Official King's Cross page lists Wed 12:00-19:00, Thu-Fri 12:00-20:00, Sat-Sun 11:00-18:00, and bank holiday Mondays 11:00-18:00. Canopy Market site says independent producers, designer-makers, artists, street food, craft drinks and music.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point walk-in still needs host check.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 as a moving market format; seating varies by stall and weather.
venues.capacity_notes: Covered Victorian steel-and-glass market hall reduces weather risk.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Camden's cleanest Market Days fit: covered, maker-led, food-led and visually coherent. It has enough structure for strangers to browse without getting lost. King's Cross gives it cultural anchors rather than pure retail sprawl.
venues.anecdote_story: The market is framed as bringing a historic market hall back to life under West Handyside Canopy.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, artisan food, designer-makers, artists, street food, craft drinks and music.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall King's Cross estate with excellent transit; still use daytime/lunch format.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min by bus/tube; within market backup allowance by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Specific trader mix varies by week; confirm market is operating on chosen date.

venues.opening_hours: Visit London lists Camden Market open seven days including bank holidays, 10:00-19:00; official site advertises Night Markets with traders open until 21:00.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a roaming group but crowd-management plan required.
venues.capacity_notes: Large multi-area market with food, retail and fixed traders; crowds are the main capacity issue.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 11:00-13:00 or Sunday 10:30-12:30; avoid Saturday peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is obvious and tourist-heavy, but still has real craft-market history, canal texture and enough food variety for a group. It works best as a curated micro-route rather than free wandering. Tier B reflects the queue/crowd penalty.
venues.anecdote_story: Official Camden Market history says Camden Lock began in 1974 as one of London's first crafts and antiques markets in the former Dingwall builders yard.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, drinks, vintage, crafts, retail stalls and canal-side market lanes.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall and crowded; use daytime slot, explicit meeting point and buddy system.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min by transit; within market backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Obviousness and crowding reduce PAINTS intimacy; do not run peak Saturday.

venues.opening_hours: Locations Hub describes 77 pitches open Thursdays and Saturdays all day; Wikipedia records Thursday/Saturday market days and long market history. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current trader volume before publishing.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a neighbourhood walk-and-taste format, not as a polished producer market.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor street market; weather and current stall count are risks.
venues.why_fits_paints: The appeal is community history rather than curated design. It gives PAINTS a less obvious Camden counterweight, but the current market identity is not as strong as Canopy or Camden Lock. Tier B only.
venues.anecdote_story: Queen's Crescent is one of Camden's older street markets, with sources tracing it back to the late 19th century and earlier street-trading regulation.
venues.serve_or_on_view: General market stalls, food, household goods and community traders.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; use Kentish Town West/Gospel Oak routes and avoid lingering after market close.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by bus/tube; within market backup allowance by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: May feel too utilitarian and under-curated if stall count is low; host must scout first.

venues.venue_name: The Regent's Park: Queen Mary's Gardens and Queen Elizabeth II Garden route
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks lists pedestrian gates 05:00-21:30 in summer; 2026 opening-times FAQ points to official weekly opening page. Host must confirm exact gate times for event date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; no booking for route. Meeting/weather backup walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Use a compact 90-minute loop: Queen Mary's Gardens, Inner Circle, new Queen Elizabeth II Garden, lake edge.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 10:30-12:30; finish before lunch crowds.
venues.why_fits_paints: Regent's Park is on PAINTS Premium Launch Parks list, so it clears the Creative Walk safety authority. The route has colour, landscape design and a new biodiversity story without needing Hampstead Heath. It is accessible, central and easy to back up in bad weather.
venues.anecdote_story: Stops: Queen Mary's Gardens rose beds; John Nash's planned landscape; the 2026 Queen Elizabeth II Garden biodiversity project; the lake/wetland edge. The Guardian reported the new garden turned a former brownfield horticultural site into an 8,000 sq m wildlife habitat.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, rose gardens, new biodiversity garden, wetland/lake edge.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Premium Launch Park. Daytime route only; exit before dusk gate closure.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within approx. 5 min of Inner Circle start; host must verify current cafe hours and group walk-in.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and gate closure times; backup cafe is operationally weaker than the walk itself.

venues.venue_name: Primrose Hill viewpoint and Chalcot Crescent route
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks says Primrose Hill is open 24 hours on weekdays, with summer weekend closures 22:00-06:00 from Friday 3 April to Sunday 8 November.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park in permitted hours; meeting/weather backup walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime 8-12; not recommended after dusk.
venues.capacity_notes: Use a short route: Chalk Farm approach, Chalcot Crescent colour, summit view, Regent's Park edge.
venues.why_fits_paints: Primrose Hill is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist, with a safety caveat. The view and surrounding streets are excellent for visual prompts, but recent safety reporting makes this daytime-only. Tier B is the honest score.
venues.anecdote_story: Royal Parks notes Primrose Hill was purchased from Eton College in 1841 to extend open-air recreation for north London, and that it once had a lively reputation with duels and prize-fights.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Skyline view, hilltop, Chalcot Crescent architecture, Regent's Park edge.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist, not Premium. Recent 2026 press reported serious crime concerns in Royal Parks/Primrose Hill; run daytime only, avoid summer late evenings.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from Chalcot/Regent's Park Road start; walk-in/group policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.risks_watchouts: Safety caveat and weekend night closures; never use as evening walk.

venues.venue_name: Regent's Canal: Camden Lock to King's Cross / Angel daytime route
venues.opening_hours: Canal towpath route; PAINTS v6 permits Regent's Canal Camden to Angel as a managed urban daytime-only canal walk. Host must confirm no towpath closures.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a walking group if kept moving and daytime; narrower towpath sections require single-file pacing.
venues.capacity_notes: Stops: Camden Lock market history; canal/industrial edge; Granary Square/Coal Drops; Angel tunnel/towpath interpretation if continuing east.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:00-12:00 or Friday 11:00-13:00; avoid dark and peak towpath commuter times.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is an explicitly permitted PAINTS canal stretch and gives Camden a route with water, industry and urban craft texture. It works as a daytime observation walk with three to four structured stops. Tier B because towpaths are narrower and weather backup is imperfect.
venues.anecdote_story: Canal & River Trust notes Regent's Canal was named after the Prince Regent, later George IV, and opened in 1820.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Canal boats, locks, railway/canal industrial fabric, Camden Lock, Granary Square.
venues.safety_notes: Permitted PAINTS non-park route, daytime only. Keep group together and avoid isolated stretches after dark.
venues.backup_venue: The Hawley Arms / Camden Market covered food areas
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3-5 min from Camden Lock start; group walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.risks_watchouts: Towpath crowding, rain and dark conditions; host must check Canal & River Trust notices.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Wed-Sun 11:00-18:00; exhibitions free. Current exhibition Donald Locke: Resistant Forms runs 10 Apr-30 Aug 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery; walk-in generally accepted, but group 8-12 and cafe status are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 slow-looking format; confirm group visit expectations.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery, cafe, garden and shop; cafe has had closure notices historically, so verify.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is the most PAINTS-coded gallery in Camden: quiet, serious, free and slightly out of the obvious central-museum lane. Donald Locke gives craft/sculptural texture rather than a generic white-cube visit. The garden/cafe/shop make pacing natural.
venues.anecdote_story: The centre is a free contemporary art gallery in Hampstead with a programme of exhibitions, residencies and courses; the current File Note is published for Donald Locke: Resistant Forms.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Donald Locke: Resistant Forms, 10 Apr-30 Aug 2026; contemporary art, sculpture/forms, garden and shop.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime/early evening only; route from Finchley Road & Frognal or Hampstead along main streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min by Google Maps walking search; verify current exhibitions/hours before use.
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe and group walk-in status must be confirmed; otherwise use as gallery-only event.

venues.opening_hours: Official programme says galleries and Reading Room Tue-Sun 10:00-18:00, library Mon-Fri 10:00-18:00 and Sat 10:00-16:00, shop/cafe daily 10:00-18:00. Homepage notes closure Mon 6 and Tue 7 July 2026, reopening Wed 8 July.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum/gallery; walk-in likely for public galleries, but group 8-12 and event-date access are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if split briefly in galleries then reconvene in Reading Room/cafe.
venues.capacity_notes: Large public museum; cafe and Reading Room support pacing, but exhibitions can be sensitive in theme.
venues.why_fits_paints: Wellcome is rich in story and human-scale prompts, not just objects. The 2026 programme around ageing, care, tenderness and mental health gives strong conversation material. It is robust and free, though less hidden than PAINTS' ideal.
venues.anecdote_story: Wellcome describes itself as a free museum and library exploring health and human experience.
venues.serve_or_on_view: The Coming of Age, Tenderness and Rage, and from 10 July 2026 Audrey Amiss and Rudy Loewe exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Euston Road is busy and transit-rich; avoid late night and use Euston/Warren Street main routes.
venues.backup_venue: Grant Museum of Zoology / UCL museums area
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8-10 min by Google Maps walking search; verify July 2026 opening and group access.
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed 6-7 July 2026; some themes may need content framing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Sat 10:00-17:00, Sun 11:00-17:00, Mon closed; amended 2026 closures include 23 July opening at 11:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed museum; walk-in/day tickets for group 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Use off-peak weekday.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for quiet guided-looking group; confirm admission/group policy.
venues.capacity_notes: Smaller museum; best with timed host entry and clear meet point.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Foundling Museum has a rare balance of intimate history, art objects and moral conversation. The 2026 displays on voices/tokens and Handel-era culture suit PAINTS' story-led format. It feels more particular than a blockbuster museum.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum tells the story of the Foundling Hospital, the UK's first children's charity and first public art gallery; current events include talks on Foundling tokens.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Voices in the Room, 16 Jun-1 Nov 2026; 300 Years of the Rival Queens: Handel's 1726 Opera Season, 30 Jun-25 Oct 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Brunswick Square/Russell Square daytime route is appropriate; do not use adjacent non-approved parks as Creative Walk meeting-point anchors.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticket/group walk-in needs confirmation; Monday closed and 23 July amended opening.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists current exhibition 2 July-15 August 2026; public opening hours need host confirmation because the scraped page did not expose a stable hours block.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery/drop-in for exhibitions likely, but walk-in/group 8-12 and hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 if open; small gallery requires quiet pacing.
venues.capacity_notes: Use as a 60-75 minute gallery visit paired with nearby independent coffee beforehand.
venues.why_fits_paints: October Gallery gives Camden a non-institutional, international contemporary art pick with textiles, ceramics, painting and photography in the current show. It is intimate and story-rich rather than blockbuster. The only reason it is not higher is hours/walk-in uncertainty.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery's July 2026 exhibition pairs Zimbabwean ceramicist Xanthe Somers with Moroccan painter Yacout Hamdouch, plus a James Barnor photography spotlight.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Xanthe Somers and Yacout Hamdouch: Poetic Threads; Spotlight: James Barnor, 2 July-15 August 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Bloomsbury daytime route is suitable; use Russell Square/Holborn main streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Must verify opening hours and whether 8-12 can arrive without booking.

venues.venue_name: British Museum: Admonitions Scroll / works-on-paper focused visit
venues.neighbourhood_station: Russell Square / Holborn / Tottenham Court Road
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists daily 10:00-17:00, Fridays until 20:30, last entry 16:45/20:15; special exhibitions daily 10:00-17:00 and Fridays until 20:30. Admonitions Scroll reopens 13 July-24 August 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free entry but booking online recommended; timed/special access must be confirmed for event date. Tier B because of crowds, booking friction and obviousness.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally possible, but group should be split into viewing pairs in busy rooms.
venues.capacity_notes: Use a precise object-led route, not a general museum wander.
venues.why_fits_paints: The object story is exceptional, but the venue is the definition of obvious. It qualifies only as a tightly curated works-on-paper visit around the Admonitions Scroll or quieter display rooms. Avoid using it as a generic British Museum day.
venues.anecdote_story: The Admonitions Scroll is displayed only for six weeks a year due to conservation precautions, giving the visit a strong time-bound story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Admonitions of the Instructress to the Court Ladies, 13 July-24 August 2026; museum collections and selected displays.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall central Bloomsbury; safe main-route transit but pick clear meeting point outside crowds.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Crowds and booking friction; current Admonitions display starts after run date, on 13 July 2026.

venues.venue_name: London Review Bookshop with St. JOHN at the Bookshop
venues.opening_hours: Bookshop official site lists Mon-Sat 10:00-18:30, Sun 12:00-18:00. St. JOHN at the Bookshop lists Mon-Sat 08:00-18:30 and Sun 12:00-18:00, with extended evening hours for events.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe walk-in likely for individuals; 8-12 seating for two hours is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Use off-peak and be prepared to browse in pairs.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best Books & Coffee fit in Camden, but group seating must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop plus next-door/in-house St. JOHN cafe; small terrace/cafe atmosphere reported by press.
venues.why_fits_paints: It exactly satisfies the raised Books & Coffee rule: serious independent bookshop plus a real cafe partner inside/next door. The literary identity is deep, and St. JOHN adds a food story rather than generic coffee. It is obvious to literary London but still the category standout.
venues.anecdote_story: Wallpaper reported the St. JOHN partnership opened in 2025 after the previous London Review Cake Shop closed, pairing the LRB bookshop community with St. JOHN's simple bakery/cafe ethos.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent books, events programme, St. JOHN pastries, coffee, tea, hot chocolate, lunch and wine/snacks around events.
venues.safety_notes: Central Bloomsbury, close to Holborn/Russell Square; good daytime route.
venues.backup_venue: Housmans Bookshop plus nearby independent coffee only as cultural fallback
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying bookshop+cafe backup within 10 min; closest strong literary alternative is Housmans near King's Cross, over 10 min by transit/walk combination. Primary remains Tier A because it is unusually robust, but host should not rely on a like-for-like backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: No strict same-category backup within 10 min; 8-12 two-hour seating requires confirmation.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Not on the PAINTS v6 approved park/route safety list. The brief explicitly says famous London parks not on the list must be treated as excluded and not sourced.
Excellent floating bookshop and PAINTS-coded story, but no verified on-site or literally next-door independent coffee seating suitable for a two-hour 8-12 group. Fails raised Books & Coffee rule.
Strong independent radical bookshop, but no verified on-site or literal next-door cafe/coffee partner with seating. Kept as cultural fallback only, not a qualifying Books & Coffee venue.
Excellent second-hand bookshop but no verified on-site/literal next-door independent coffee partner that satisfies the PAINTS Books & Coffee rule.
Historic street market, but current sources describe limited utilitarian stalls and weak curated producer/maker atmosphere; below PAINTS score floor.
Historically strong Camden institution, but the Camden museum building closed in 2023 and current operations are not a regular walk-in museum/gallery day fit.
Large bookshop with cafe, but chain status conflicts with PAINTS independent/no-chain aesthetic for Books & Coffee.
Strong Camden lore but too obvious/celebrity-coded and no verified group walk-in seating for 8-12; better as weather backup for canal start than primary PAINTS drinks pick.
